classes.name: millenniumdb_driver.millenniumdb_error.MillenniumDBError
documentation: This class represents an error that has been thrown by the driver

classes.name: millenniumdb_driver.millenniumdb_error.ResultError
documentation:
This specific error is thrown when an error occurs while processing a result
in order to be able to consume the results obtained so far

:ivar result: The result object that was being processed
:vartype result: Result

methods.name: millenniumdb_driver.iobuffer.IOBuffer.pop_uint16
documentation:
---
return: The uint16 popped from the end of the used buffer, removing its used bytes.
---

The class IOBuffer can be used to read and write data to and from a binary buffer.


Data itself should not be manipulated appart from extend method.


Data view is what will be sliced, written and read


:ivar view: The memoryview of the buffer.
:vartype view: memoryview
:ivar num_used_bytes: The number of used bytes in the buffer.
:vartype num_used_bytes: int

classes.name: millenniumdb_driver.graph_objects.GraphPathSegment

methods.name: millenniumdb_driver.graph_objects.GraphPathSegment.__init__
documentation:
attributes:
from_ (object): The starting node of the segment
to (object): The ending node of the segment
type (object): The type of the segment

Represents a segment in the path.


Whether the segment is in reverse direction,
for example the segment should be printed
as:

`(to)<-[type]-(from)` instead of `(from)-[type]->(to)`

:ivar from_: The starting node of the segment.
:vartype from_: object
:ivar to: The ending node of the segment.
:vartype to: object
:ivar type: The type of the segment.
:vartype type: object
:ivar reverse: Whether the segment is in reverse direction.
:vartype reverse: bool
